text: HORMUZ UPDATE: US has blocked 70+ Iranian tankers from reaching ports — 166M barrel capacity worth ~$13B per Army Recognition. Qatari LNG tanker Al Kharaitiyat attempting first Hormuz transit since war began. Iran approved passage as confidence-building measure with Pakistan mediator. IRGC Navy warned any tanker attack triggers 'heavy assault on American centers.'
verificationNote: Bloomberg, CNBC, and Army Recognition confirmed tanker blockade and Qatari transit attempt.

text: IRAN DEAL STATUS: Tehran still has not formally responded to US 14-point MOU. FM spokesman Baghaei: 'We don't pay attention to deadlines or timing.' Rubio-Witkoff met Qatar PM in Miami to push back-channel pressure. Multiple mediators active: Qatar, Pakistan, Oman. S&P 500 hit record 7,398.93 on deal optimism despite no concrete progress.
verificationNote: Axios confirmed Miami meeting. TASS and Fox News confirmed Baghaei quotes.

text: SANCTIONS EXPANSION: US State Dept sanctioned 11 entities + 3 individuals in China, Belarus, UAE for aiding Iran's military. Key target: 3 Chinese satellite imagery firms (Meentropy Technology, The Earth Eye, Chang Guang Satellite) provided targeting data enabling Iranian strikes on US forces. China deployed anti-sanctions law for first time ordering citizens NOT to comply with US sanctions on 5 Chinese refineries.
verificationNote: State Department press release and Bloomberg confirmed sanctions details. Al Jazeera confirmed China anti-sanctions law deployment.

text: LEBANON FRONT: 36 killed in Israeli airstrikes in past 24 hours per Lebanese Health Ministry — total 2,715+ killed since March 2. IDF struck 85+ Hezbollah targets incl. underground Beqaa Valley weapons facility. Evacuation warnings for 9 villages near Tyre. Hezbollah drones wounded 3 IDF reservists at Misgav Am/Shlomi (1 severely). Lebanon ceasefire fully collapsed.
verificationNote: Haaretz confirmed 36 killed figure. Jerusalem Post confirmed IDF strikes on 85+ targets. Times of Israel confirmed IDF casualties.

text: IRGC THREAT ESCALATION: Aerospace Force Gen. Mousavi says missiles and drones are 'locked on American targets in the region and aggressor enemy ships — awaiting order to fire.' IRGC Navy warns any attack on Iranian tankers triggers 'heavy assault on American centers.' Mokhber calls Hormuz control 'as precious as atomic bomb.' Internal IRGC-Pezeshkian split continues.
verificationNote: Tribune India, Al Jazeera, and RT confirmed IRGC statements.

text: OIL & MARKETS: Brent $101.29 (+26.9%), WTI $95.42 (+26.4%) — weekly loss ~6-7% despite Friday gains. S&P 500 new record 7,398.93 — 6th straight winning week. Gas $4.53 (AAA, slight decline from $4.55 peak). PolitiFact rated Trump claim gas prices falling as FALSE. Wheat $6.065/bu (+16.2% YoY). FAO food index 130.7 — highest since Feb 2023.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed S&P record. AAA confirmed gas prices. PolitiFact confirmed fact-check. Trading Economics confirmed wheat.

text: NAVAL OPS: US F/A-18 from USS Bush fired precision munitions into smokestacks of Iranian tankers M/T Sea Star III and M/T Sevda — both disabled. 2nd and 3rd Iranian ships hit in 3 days. IRGC Navy seized Barbados-registered tanker Ocean Koi. Satellite imagery shows 45-71 sq km oil spill near Kharg Island — Iran denies, calls it 'psychological warfare.' Fox News reveals alleged secret Iranian nuclear 'Rainbow Site' in Semnan Province.
verificationNote: The War Zone confirmed tanker strikes. Al Jazeera confirmed Ocean Koi seizure. Anadolu Agency confirmed Kharg oil spill. Iran International confirmed Rainbow Site report.
